regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, winds are light from the southeast at 4 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation with no expected weather delays. Looking ahead over the next 24 to 48 hours, southwest winds will increase to 13 knots and skies will become overcast with a 28% rain probability, which may affect shore excursions in Miami and nearby Port Everglades. By day two, winds shift west at 10 knots with continued overcast conditions and a 36% chance of rain, potentially impacting Port Everglades and Port Canaveral operations. Beyond 48 hours, conditions improve with south-southwest winds at 13 knots, clear skies, and a 13% rain probability, suggesting favorable weather for cruising out of Miami and surrounding ports.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, winds out of the south at 17 knots will bring thunderstorms with a 24% rain probability around Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini, which may affect shore excursions and some port operations. Current conditions show southeast winds at 9 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing for smooth embarkation and disembarkation early in the day. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southwest winds at 11 knots and a 55% chance of rain showers could impact port activity in Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Puerto Plata, potentially delaying some outdoor excursions. Beyond 48 hours, winds remain southerly at 10 knots with a 47% chance of rain showers expected to affect La Romana and Santo Domingo, suggesting continued caution for open-air activities and ship maneuvering. Crew and passengers should monitor updates closely as conditions evolve over the coming days.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, an elevated 54% rain probability with drizzle is expected to affect port operations and shore excursions in San Juan and nearby ports such as St. Thomas and St. Croix, where ESE winds at 11 knots will prevail. Current conditions show SE winds at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and a lower 17% rain probability, indicating improving conditions during embarkation activities early in the day. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, drizzle with a 10% rain probability and steady ESE winds at 11 knots will continue to impact St. Maarten and Antigua, possibly causing light delays or adjustments to outdoor excursions. Beyond 48 hours, winds will remain from the ESE at 12 knots with drizzle continuing and an 18% rain probability, suggesting that cruise operations should monitor for light moisture but expect generally manageable conditions in St. Kitts and surrounding ports. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for intermittent wet weather and moderate trade winds throughout the region.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing light southerly winds at 7 knots with drizzle expected and a 64% rain probability, which is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. Costa Maya and Belize City are likely to see similar conditions, with elevated chances of rain affecting outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds around 6 knots will bring rain showers with an 85% rain probability to Cozumel and Roatan, resulting in more significant disruptions to embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to the east-southeast at 6 knots with drizzle and a 38% rain probability in Cozumel, suggesting improving but still damp conditions that may affect some shore excursions. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for wet weather and possible delays through the next couple of days.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, expect elevated easterly winds near 21 knots across Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability keeping visibility good for safe embarkation and disembarkation. These steady 21-knot winds may cause some moderate sea chop, so shore excursions in Aruba and Curaçao should proceed with caution, especially water-based activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds will hold steady at 21 knots with partly cloudy skies in Aruba and similar conditions expected in Curacao and Bonaire; rain chances remain below 10%, so outdoor plans should remain largely unaffected. By day three, expect a slight increase to 22-knot easterly winds along with drizzle in Aruba, while Cartagena should see comparable wind speeds but clearer skies, so vessels should prepare for increased wind-driven sea conditions affecting docking and tender operations. Passengers and crew should monitor for these sustained elevated winds as they may influence maneuvering near port and impact smaller boat transfers through the period.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, expect east winds around 10 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability in Barbados, allowing smooth port operations and shore excursions without weather disruption. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will strengthen slightly to 13 knots with drizzle and rain probabilities increasing to 22% and 26%, respectively, which may affect outdoor activities in Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique. By day three, sustained east winds near 14 knots combined with an 84% rain probability and persistent drizzle are expected to impact port operations and shore excursions significantly in Barbados and neighboring ports like St. Vincent and Grenada. Cruise vessels should prepare for reduced visibility and wet conditions during embarkation and disembarkation in these locations. Beyond 48 hours, the trend toward elevated drizzle and steady east winds suggests continued wet weather, so crews and passengers should plan accordingly for potential operational adjustments.
regions.los-angeles: Today, winds are light from the north-northwest at 3 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations at Los Angeles, San Pedro, Long Beach, and Catalina Island. Through the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds shifting to the west at 10 knots on day one and then to the southwest at 10 knots on day two, with persistent overcast conditions and less than 10% rain probability across all ports, maintaining favorable conditions for shore excursions and port activities. Beyond 48 hours, winds will ease slightly to 9 knots from the south as skies become partly cloudy, continuing to support seamless cruise operations without rain concerns. Passengers and crew can anticipate stable marine and port conditions throughout this period.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, light east-northeast winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies will support smooth port operations and comfortable conditions for shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos, with less than 10% rain probability. regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, winds are calm from the northeast at 0 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing excellent conditions for embarkation and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Overall, stable marine conditions and low rain chances support seamless cruise operations in the region through midweek.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, including Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, conditions are ideal for port operations and shore excursions with light SSE winds at 3 knots, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability. Visibility is excellent, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southwest winds increasing slightly to 7 knots with continued clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for all three ports. Looking beyond 48 hours, winds shift to SSW at 8 knots accompanied by overcast skies while rain probability remains under 10%, so cruise operations should continue without weather-related disruptions. Passengers and crew can anticipate stable marine conditions throughout this period.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today, thunderstorms with a 14% rain probability are expected to impact Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, with southwest winds at 11 knots potentially causing brief disruptions to shore excursions and port operations. Current conditions show light north-northeast winds at 2 knots and clear skies, presenting calm conditions for embarkation and disembarkation early in the day. Beyond 48 hours, steady west-southwest winds near 10 knots and continued clear skies will support smooth port operations and excursions throughout the region. regions.western-mediterranean: Today, moderate southeast winds at 7 knots and a 55% chance of rain showers may affect port operations and shore excursions in Barcelona and nearby ports such as Valencia and Palma de Mallorca. Expect somewhat reduced visibility with partly cloudy skies shifting to more overcast conditions during these rain showers. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will veer to the north-northeast at 11 knots, with rain showers dropping to less than 10% rain probability, improving conditions for embarkation and disembarkation at Malaga, Cadiz, and Gibraltar. Beyond 48 hours, increasing north winds at 12 knots will bring a 79% chance of thunderstorms, which is expected to impact port activities in Lisbon and surrounding areas, requiring heightened awareness for safety and possible excursion adjustments. Passengers and crew should prepare for these developing weather changes as the thunderstorm threat increases.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, thunderstorms with an 83% rain probability are ongoing in Naples, bringing WSW winds at 5 knots that are expected to create delays in embarkation and disembarkation operations along the Campania coast, including Naples and Civitavecchia. Visibility will be reduced, and shore excursions in Naples, Livorno, and La Spezia are expected to be impacted by wet conditions and frequent lightning. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will increase from the southwest to 7-9 knots with thunderstorm coverage persisting at 98% rain probability, affecting Genoa, Monaco, and Marseille with significant disruptions to port operations and outdoor activities. By day three, thunderstorms will continue with a slightly reduced 77% rain probability and SSW winds at 9 knots, extending impacts toward Sardinia and Corsica, where intermittent heavy rain and gusty winds will require caution during all ship-to-shore movements. Passengers and crew should anticipate ongoing operational adjustments due to these persistent convective conditions throughout the Central Mediterranean.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, winds are light from the southwest at 4 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing smooth port operations and shore excursions in Athens, Piraeus, and nearby ports like Santorini and Mykonos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift slightly to SSW at 7 to 10 knots with persistent overcast conditions and less than 10% rain probability across the Eastern Mediterranean, including Rhodes and Istanbul, maintaining stable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation. By the third day, winds will return to SSW at 8 knots with continued overcast skies and minimal rain chance, supporting ongoing cruise ship maneuvering near Izmir, Cyprus, and Haifa without weather-related delays. regions.southeast-alaska: Today, Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway will experience an increasing rain threat with Juneau shifting to east winds at 5 knots and a 100% chance of rain, which is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. Currently, skies are overcast with light north-northeast winds at 2 knots and less than 10% rain probability, allowing for smooth morning embarkation in Seattle. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, steady east-southeast winds near 7 knots will continue to bring rain with 100% probability to these primary ports, likely causing wet conditions for outdoor activities. By day three, rain chances slightly diminish to 90% with drizzle expected and east-southeast winds easing to 5 knots, signaling persistent damp weather but improved visibility for cruising near Sitka and Haines. Passengers should prepare for ongoing wet conditions affecting vessel operations and shore excursions throughout this period.
